steps.title: Condition for no solution
steps.content: An equation has no solution when both sides have the same $x$-coefficient (same slope) but different constant terms. This creates a contradiction like $-\frac{1}{9} = \frac{1}{9}$, which is never true.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content: The left side is $-13x - \frac{1}{9}$. For no solution, the right side must also have coefficient $-13$ on $x$ but a different constant.

steps.title: Identify the no-solution case
steps.content: Choice B: $-13x - \frac{1}{9} = -13x + \frac{1}{9}$. Cancel $-13x$ from both sides: $-\frac{1}{9} = \frac{1}{9}$. This is false for all values of $x$ — no solution.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$-13x - \frac{1}{9} = -13x - \frac{1}{9}$': Identical on both sides — true for all $x$ (infinitely many solutions). ✗
  • Choice B — '$-13x - \frac{1}{9} = -13x + \frac{1}{9}$': Same $x$-coefficient, different constant. Simplifies to $-\frac{1}{9} = \frac{1}{9}$, a contradiction. No solution. ✓
  • Choice C — '$-13x - \frac{1}{9} = 13x - \frac{1}{9}$': Different slopes; simplifies to $-26x = 0$, so $x = 0$. One solution. ✗
  • Choice D — '$-13x - \frac{1}{9} = 13x + \frac{1}{9}$': Different slopes; simplifies to $-26x = \frac{2}{9}$, so $x = -\frac{1}{117}$. One solution. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
